mnemonics.title: FUNCTIONS Signature Rules
mnemonics.content: FUNCTIONS: F-Unique signatures, N-Number of params, C-Const can differentiate, T-Type of params, I-Order matters, O-Only params (not return), N-Name same, S-Signature determines call

acronyms.expansion: Function Overloading Signature - parameters only, not return type
acronyms.usage: Remember: Return type is NOT part of overload resolution

acronyms.expansion: Promotion Exact Match - compiler prefers exact match over promotion
acronyms.usage: When resolving overloaded calls, exact match > promotion > conversion

visualTips.visualization: Draw a flowchart: Call → Check exact match → Check promotion → Check conversion → If multiple equally good → Ambiguity error
Use 3 circles: [Number] + [Type] + [Order] = Signature. Return type is OUTSIDE - it's NOT part!

analogies.analogy: Like a person named 'John' who is a doctor (Dr. John), teacher (Mr. John), and father (Father John). Same name, different role/identity based on context - like function name same, parameters define which one is called.
Like a restaurant menu - all options (overloaded functions) are listed (compiled) and customer (function call) chooses one based on order (arguments). Decision is made before serving (runtime).
